title: Red Hat Bug Fix Advisory: cifs-utils bug fix update
overview: An update for cifs-utils is now available for Red Hat Enterprise Linux 10.
description: The SMB/CIFS protocol is a standard file sharing protocol widely deployed on Microsoft Windows machines. The cifs-utils packages contain tools for mounting shares on Linux using the SMB/CIFS protocol. The tools in this package work in conjunction with support in the kernel to allow one to mount a SMB/CIFS share onto a client and use it as if it were a standard Linux file system.Bug Fix(es): [rhel-10] cifs-utils need update to 7.2 to adapt latest kernel CIFS update (JIRA:RHEL-80183)

full_text:
An update for cifs-utils is now available for Red Hat Enterprise Linux 10.

The SMB/CIFS protocol is a standard file sharing protocol widely deployed on Microsoft Windows machines. The cifs-utils packages contain tools for mounting shares on Linux using the SMB/CIFS protocol. The tools in this package work in conjunction with support in the kernel to allow one to mount a SMB/CIFS share onto a client and use it as if it were a standard Linux file system.Bug Fix(es): [rhel-10] cifs-utils need update to 7.2 to adapt latest kernel CIFS update (JIRA:RHEL-80183)
